english_text:
SOUL
BODY
NAMED (John Doe)
Spiritual ATTRIBUTES (Sifaat) — brave, kind, Doctor, patient
Physical ATTRIBUTES (Sifaat) — Slim, Dark, Handsome, Tall
alt_text: Teaching diagram contrasting Soul and Body. Two human silhouettes (yellow on left = Soul, black on right = Body), both labelled 'ZAAT (noun)' and connected by a 'NAMED (John Doe)' arrow. Spiritual attributes (brave, kind, Doctor, patient) point to the soul; physical attributes (Slim, Dark, Handsome, Tall) point to the body. Bottom green box: 'ک + مثلہ' (the Quranic ka- + mithlihi).
notes: Pedagogical setup for the kalam discussion of how the Quranic phrase 'laysa ka-mithlihi shay' (Q42:11) negates both substance (zat) and attribute (sifa) likeness between God and creatures.
